title: Smt.Leelavathi G vs Sri.Vinod Kumar on 18 December, 2015
body: Smt.Leelavathi G vs Sri.Vinod Kumar on 18 December, 2015 IN THE COURT OF THE XXIV ADDL.CITY CIVIL & SESSIONS JUDGE BANGALORE CITY (C.C.H.No.6) This the 18th day of December, 2015 Present: Sri. S.SRIDHARA, B.Sc.,LL.B., th 24 Addl. City Civil & Sessions Judge, Bangalore City. O. S. No.6477/2015 PLAINTIFFS: 1. Smt.Leelavathi G W/o B.R.Vijayashekar, Aged about 50 years. 2. Sri. V.Niranjana Tejaswi S/o B.R.Vijayasekhar, Aged about 26 years. Both are R/at No.133/A, 1st Floor, 3rd Main Road, Gandhinagar, Bengaluru- 560 032. (By Sri. M.T.Nanaiah, Advocate) Vs. DEFENDANT: Sri.Vinod Kumar S/o Sri. H.Thimmaiah, Aged about 23 years, R/at 24, 3rd Cross, SR Layout, Bonemill, Hesarabhatta Main Road, Bengaluru- 560 073. Also at No.133/A, Front Portion 2 O.S.6477/2015 Ground Floor, 3rd Main Road, Ganganagara, Bengaluru- 560 032. (Exparte.) Date of institution of the suit: 24.07.2015 Nature of the suit: Ejectment Date of commencement of 28.09.2015 recording of evidence: Date on which Judgment was 18.12.2015 pronounced: Duration: Days Months Years 24 04 00 JUDGMENT This is a suit filed by the plaintiffs against the defendant to direct the defendant to quit and deliver the vacant possession of the suit schedule premises forthwith; to direct the defendant to pay a sum of Rs.1,20,000/- being the arrears of rentals due for a period of six months from 1.1.2015 to 30.6.2015 at the rate of Rs.20,000/- per month with interest at 12% p.a.; to direct the defendant to pay damages at the rate of 3 O.S.6477/2015 Rs.40,000/- per month from the date of the suit until handing over the vacant possession and to hold enquiry with regard to mesne profit from the date of suit as contemplated Under Order 20 Rule 12 of CPC and for costs and such other reliefs. 2. a) The plaintiffs have stated that they are the lawful owners-cum-landlord of the residential premises morefully described in the schedule and referred to as schedule premises. The defendant was a tenant under the plaintiffs (till the date of termination of the tenancy) on a monthly rent of Rs.20,000/- as per the lease deed dated 15.10.2014 for a period of 11 months. The original lease deed is with the defendant. However plaintiffs will call upon the defendant at appropriate stage of the suit to produce the original lease agreement. Plaintiffs also used to issue rental receipt on receiving the rent and accordingly counterfoils are also produced. b) According to the plaintiffs, the tenancy is a monthly tenancy as per English Calendar Month and rent 4 O.S.6477/2015 was required to be paid on or before 5th day of succeeding month without any default. According to the plaintiffs, the defendant is in arrears of rentals from 1.1.2015 till 30.6.2015 for a period of six months at the rate of Rs.20,000/- per month amounting to Rs.1,20,000/-. Despite repeated requests and demands through telephone call as well as personal visit of the plaintiffs, the defendant went on postponing the payment of arrears of rentals for one or the other reasons to have illegal gain for himself and to cause wrongful loss to the plaintiffs etc. As such, plaintiffs are claiming the outstanding payment of Rs.1,20,000/- in the suit. c) According to the plaintiffs, the defendant approached the plaintiff on 6.2.2015 and he has expressed his willingness to vacate the premises voluntarily on or before 11.3.2015 since he has taken an alternative premises. However the defendant has requested the plaintiffs for refund of Rs.50,000/- out of the security deposit and accordingly the plaintiffs have 5 O.S.6477/2015 paid Rs.50,000/- on 7.3.2015 through Cheque bearing No.918238 dated 9.3.2015 and the same has been encashed by the defendant on 9.3.2015 from Canara Bank, Nandi Durga Road, Jayamahal Extension, Bengaluru. The defendant also issued receipt for having received the said sum and that receipt is also produced. The defendant has not vacated the schedule premises as agreed. Though the defendant agreed in principle to quit and deliver the premises in a couple of months went on postponing to vacate the premises on one pretext or the other. The defendant did not comply with the legitimate demand made by the plaintiff. The defendant has not paid the rentals as and when it becomes due. d) Plaintiffs also further stated that they do not want to keep an undesirable tenant like the defendant and in the circumstances tenancy came to be terminated by the plaintiffs by issuing the legal notice dated 6.6.2015 through their counsel terminating the tenancy of the defendant as contemplated Under Section 106 of 6 O.S.6477/2015 T.P.Act calling upon the defendants to quit and deliver the vacant possession of the premises by availing a notice period of 15 days from the date of service. The notice has been duly served on the defendant on 15.6.2015. This suit is brought for ejectment by more than 15 days after service of notice as stated and hence there is a clear 15 days notice to quit. Office copy of the legal notice, postal receipts, unserved postal cover, postal track record are also produced. e) Plaintiffs further stated that subsequent to termination of the tenancy, the defendant has no right to remain in occupation of the schedule premises and defendant is liable to pay damages by way of compensation for use and occupation/mesne profits at the rate of Rs.40,000/-, which is prevailing rate of rent in the locality for similar accommodation, if it is let out. Plaintiffs will claim future damages during the pendency of the suit and also for subsequent period till the date of delivery of the schedule premises. 7 O.S.6477/2015 f) Plaintiffs also stated that the defendant also assaulted and abused the plaintiffs on 13.3.2015 and in this regard, the 1st plaintiff filed complaint also with R.T.Nagar Polcie, Bengaluru and copy of the complaint and FIR are also produced. g) According to the plaintiffs, the cause of action for filing of this suit arose on expiry of 15 days of service of notice, which was issued Under Section 106 of the T.P.Act by legal notice dated 6.6.2015 and thereafter subsequently within the jurisdiction of this Court. Accordingly plaintiffs pray for decreeing the suit as prayed. 3. Schedule of the plaint reads as follows: he front portion of the ground floor premixes consisting two furnished Rooms, A Hall and Toilet, in property bearing No.133/A, 3rd Main Road, Ganganagar, Bengaluru- 560 032, measuring North to South 30 feet and East to West 30 feet and total area measuring 900 square feet with electric and bathroom fittings, 4 nos., ceiling fans, 4 nos. tube 8 O.S.6477/2015 lights, 4 blubs, POP ceiling and mat flooring and bounded on: East by: Private property. West by: Road. North by: Complex building and South by: House No.133. 4. Record discloses that summons issued to the defendant served personally and inspite of that, he remained absent and placed exparte on 4.9.2015. 5. On perusal of the plaint, the following points that arise for my consideration are: 1. Whether the plaintiffs prove that there was valid termination of the tenancy as contemplated Under Section 106 of T.P.Act? 2. Whether the plaintiffs further prove that they are entitled for vacant possession of the plaint schedule property from the defendant? 3. Whether the plaintiffs further prove that defendant is due to the plaintiffs to an extent of Rs.1,20,000/- towards arrears of rentals in respect of the schedule premises from 1.1.2015 to 30.6.2015 amounting to Rs.1,20,000/-? 4. What order or decree? 9 O.S.6477/2015 6. In support of the case of the plaintiffs, the 1st plaintiff is examined as PW.1 and got marked Ex.P.1 to P.12 and closed their evidence. 7. Heard the learned counsel for the plaintiffs. Learned counsel for the plaintiffs also field a memo with Xerox copy of the lease deed dated 15.10.2014 for reference of this Court and also stated in the plaint original lease agreement is with the defendant. 8. Perused the oral and documentary evidence available on record. 9. My findings on the above Points are: Point No.1: Affirmative, Point No.2: Affirmative, Point No.3: Affirmative, Point No.4: As per the final order for the following; REASONS 10. Point Nos.1 to 3: Since all these three points are interlinked with each other and require common 10 O.S.6477/2015 discussion of facts, they are taken together for discussion to avoid repetition of facts. 11. In order to prove the case of the plaintiffs, the 1st plaintiff relied upon Ex.P.1 to P.12. Ex.P.1 to P.3 are the counterfoils of the rent receipts issued by plaintiffs to the defendant on 6.11.2014, 9.12.2014, 12.1.2015 for having accepted the rentals to an extent of Rs.20,000/- per month for the months of 15th October to 31st October 2014, November 2014 and December 2014. 12. Ex.P.4 is the office copy of the legal notice dated 6.6.2015 issued by the plaintiffs to the defendant and this notice was issued by the plaintiff to the defendant both to his residential address as well as to the address of the premises, which is leased out in favour of the defendant. In this legal notice Ex.P.4, it is specifically mentioned that the defendant was agreed to pay monthly rentals of Rs.20,000/- and also stated that the defendant is irregular in payment of rentals from January 2015 to May 2015 amounting to Rs.1,00,000/-. It also indicates 11 O.S.6477/2015 the fact that the defendant also collected a sum of Rs.50,000/- from the plaintiff out of the security deposit on 7.3.2015 through cheque bearing No.918238 dated 9.3.2015 and same was encashed by the defendant on the same day as per the particulars shown in para-4 of the legal notice. This notice also clearly indicates the fact that the tenancy of the defendant has been terminated by the plaintiffs by stating that the schedule premises is required for personal use and occupation of the plaintiffs and accordingly terminated the tenancy, which is required to be terminated Under Section 106 of the T.P.Act and also called upon the defendant to quit and deliver the vacant possession of the suit property by availing the notice period of 15 days from the date of service of legal notice. In addition to that, the defendant is also called upon to pay damages at the rate of Rs.40,000/- per month for wrongful use and occupation of the schedule premises by the defendant. 12 O.S.6477/2015 13. Ex.P.5 and P.6 both are postal receipts. Ex.P.7 is unserved postal cover. It appears that this Ex.P.7 notice was sent to the defendant's address to the premises, which is leased in favour of the defendant and same was returned unserved with shara "door locked". 14. Ex.P.8 is the receipt issued by the defendant for having received Rs.50,000/- from the plaintiffs out of the security deposit through cheque. 15. Ex.P.9 is the letter written by the defendant, wherein he has agreed to quit and deliver the vacant possession of the suit property and also agreed to collect the balance deposit amount after deducting the balance of monthly rentals, electricity charges and water charges and damages if any after vacating the premises and handing over the keys etc. He also agreed that the tenancy is a monthly tenancy and lease agreement was for a period of 11 months from 15.10.2014 and also stated that he has paid monthly rentals for the months of November 2014 and December 2014 only and he has not 13 O.S.6477/2015 paid balance rentals for the months of January, February and 10 days in the month of March 2015. 16. Ex.P.10 is the bank statement of the 1st plaintiff and this document has been produced for the limited purpose to show that the defendant has encashed a sum of Rs.50,000/-, which is received by the defendant from the plaintiff out of the security deposit amount. 17. Ex.P.11 and Ex.P.12 both are postal track record in respect of the postal article No. RK2720000281N and RK2720000141N and these two documents show that both the postal articles were delivered on 15.6.2015 and 26.6.2015 respectively. These two documents have been produced just to show that the legal notices that were issued to the defendant have been duly served on the defendant (as per Ex.P.12, it is shown as if article was unserved, door locked, first intimation that was on 15.6.2015). 18. The 1st plaintiff is examined in this case as PW.1, wherein she has reiterated the plaint averments in her 14 O.S.6477/2015 chief examination affidavit. There is no reason to disbelieve the oral evidence of PW.1 in view of the documents produced as per Ex.P.1 to P.12. There is no reason to disbelieve the oral evidence of PW.1 and documentary evidence Ex.P.1 to P.12. Both oral evidence and documentary evidence goes undisputed and unchallenged. 19. As discussed earlier the defendant remained absent and placed exparte and failed to produce any material before the Court to rebut the oral and documentary evidence available on record. 20. Hence on careful perusal of the material available on record, the plaintiff successfully proved that there was valid termination of the tenancy of the defendant in respect of the suit property as contemplated Under Section 106 of the T.P.Act. Plaintiffs also proved that they are entitled for vacant possession of the plaint schedule premises from the defendant and further proved that the defendant is due to the plaintiffs to an extent of 15 O.S.6477/2015 Rs.1,20,000/- towards arrears of rentals from 1.1.2015 to 30.6.2015. Accordingly, I answer point Nos. 1 to 3 in the 'affirmative'. 21. Point No.4: In view of the findings on Point Nos.1 to 3, I proceed to pass the following: ORDER The instant suit filed by the plaintiffs against the defendant is hereby decreed with costs. Defendant is directed to quit and deliver the vacant possession of the suit premises in favour of the plaintiffs within one month from the date of this order. If the defendant failed to quit and deliver the vacant possession of the suit property within one month, then the plaintiffs are at liberty to get possession of the suit property in accordance with and in the manner known to law. Defendant is also directed to pay a sum of Rs.1,20,000/- to the plaintiffs being the 16 O.S.6477/2015 arrears of rentals in respect of the suit property from 1.1.2015 to 30.6.2015 for a period of six months at the rate of Rs.20,000/- per month. Defendant is also directed to pay damages to the plaintiffs at the rate of Rs.20,000/- per month from the date of the suit till handing over the vacant possession of the suit property in favour of the plaintiff as per the said order. There shall be a separate enquiry into the mesne profits Under Order 20 Rule 12 CPC if the circumstances so requires. (Dictated to the Judgment Writer, computerized by her and then corrected, signed and pronounced by me in Open Court on this the 18th day of December, 2015). (S.SRIDHARA) XXIV ADDL.CITY CIVIL & SESSION JUDGE, BANGALORE CITY. ANNEXURE List of witnesses examined for the plaintiff: P.W.1: Smt Leelavathi. G 17 O.S.6477/2015 List of documents marked for the plaintiff: Ex.P.1 to Receipts issued for having paid rent. P.3: Ex.P.4: Legal notice dated 6.6.2015. Ex.P.5 & Postal receipts. P.6: Ex.P.7: Legal notice issued to the schedule premises. Ex.P.8: Receipt issued by defendant for having taken amount of Rs.50,000/-. Ex.P.9: Undertaking letter issued by defendant. Ex.P.10: Bank statement of 1st plaintiff. Ex.P.11 & Document taken from internet regarding P.12: issuance of notice to the defendant. List of witnesses examined and documents marked for the defendants: Nil. (S.SRIDHARA) XXIV ADDL.CITY CIVIL & SESSION JUDGE, BANGALORE CITY.
